channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 in 10 say Putin should not be trusted: Survey
item.description: The Quinnipiac University Poll released Wednesday found 81% of American voters believe Putin should not be trusted, while Trump's administration attempts to broker a peace deal to end the conflict between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Labor unions call on Trump to boost US shipbuilding against increasing Chinese dominance
item.description: The heads of four major labor unions are calling on President Donald Trump to boost American shipbuilding and enforce tariffs and other “strong penalties” against China for its increasing dominance.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico president says her government requested US surveillance drone flights
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that surveillance drone flights by the U.S. government over Mexico are occurring in collaboration with and at the request of her government. They come as U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U.S. to be “foreign terrorist organizations.” They are involved in drug trafficking, migrant smuggling and extend their territory through violence.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s Bolsonaro slams 'vague' accusations after attempted coup indictment
item.description: Brazil’s former President Jair Bolsonaro on Wednesday denied accusations that he orchestrated a coup to stay in office and said the charges were the result of an authoritarian regime manufacturing “internal enemies.”.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to meet with Macron, Starmer next week
item.description: President Trump is set to meet next week with two key European leaders amid tensions over Trump's approach to negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: President Trump can stop Iran's march to nuclear weapons: 're-establish credible military threat', report says
item.description: A new report from the Foundation for the Defense of Democracies provides recommendations for how the Trump administration can stop Tehran's march to produce a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze halted a crucial program fighting HIV in Africa. Here's what's at stake
item.description: The freeze on U.S. foreign aid ordered by President Donald Trump has effectively halted one of the world's most successful responses to a disease.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: SMARDAN, Romania (AP) — NATO members continued their largest combat exercises of 2025 on Wednesday, testing their ability to rapidly deploy large-scale forces on the 32-nation alliance’s eastern border as worries grow over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on remote Australian beach
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that became stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Netanyahu appoints an adviser with Trump ties to lead ceasefire talks
item.description: An Israeli official said that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has appointed a close confidant to lead negotiations for the second stage of the ceasefire with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philippine village battles dengue by offering bounties for mosquitos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A village in the densely populated Philippine capital region launched a battle against dengue Wednesday by offering a token bounty to residents for captured mosquitos — dead or alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 300 deportees from US held in Panama hotel as officials try to return them to their countries
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama is detaining in a hotel nearly 300 people from various countries deported under U.S. President Donald Trump, not allowing them to leave while waiting for international authorities to organize a return to their countries.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s prosecutor-general files charges against ex-President Bolsonaro over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet has filed charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro, alleging he attempted a coup to stay in office following his 2022 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians should be able to leave Gaza if they choose: Israel envoy
item.description: Hamas is speeding up the hostage exchange with Israel, announcing six people will be released Saturday instead of three.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French bill would ban headscarves in sport; Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill to ban religious clothing and symbols, including headscarves, during competitions, saying it is discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former USAID official warns China is already looking to fill void left by paused programs
item.description: China is reportedly trying to fill the voids left in several countries by the Trump administration's freeze of USAID funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN human rights chief accuses Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing, recruiting children
item.description: The U.N. human rights chief accused Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing children and attacking hospitals and warehouses storing humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French lawmakers mull banning headscarves in sport. Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill this week that would ban headscarves in all sporting competitions. The bill is backed by right-wing senators and will be debated from Tuesday in the upper house of the French parliament.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels tighten their grip on second major city in eastern Congo
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels tightened their grip on Bukavu on Monday, a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo whose residents appeared resigned to their fate under the new rulers.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL CINAMULA and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior American and Russian officials, including the countries’ top diplomats, will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine, officials said Monday, in what would be the most significant meeting between the sides since Moscow's full-
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JUSTIN SPIKE and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 18 killed in stampede at New Delhi railway station in India as Hindu pilgrims travel to festival
item.description: In New Delhi, India, at least 18 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station. Many victims were Hindu pilgrims traveling to the Maha Kumbh festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation
